title: peganum harmala var. harmala
intro: Peganum harmala var. harmala, also known as Syrian rue, is a plant native to the Middle East and South Asia, belonging to the Peganum genus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to induce purging, induce sweating, and act as an emetic due to the presence of harmine and other alkaloids. The health benefits of Syrian rue include its potential use in treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Syrian rue is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be smoked or used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
